model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Louisville is the safer pick because its full-season predictive profile is clearly stronger, ranking around the top 20 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik while South Florida sits closer to the top 50. The Cardinals rate better on both offense and defense, and the betting market also leans their way as a solid favorite. South Florida has a credible upset path if Louisville’s backcourt health limits its offense or the Bulls win a faster, higher-variance game, but Louisville still looks like the more complete team.

reasoningStep.title: Back Louisville over South Florida
reasoningStep.summary: Louisville has the stronger two-way efficiency profile and market support, though an injury question in its backcourt keeps confidence from being higher.
evidence: Louisville ratings: KenPom No. 18 (AdjEM +25.42, AdjO 124.1, AdjD 98.7); Torvik No. 16 (AdjO 124.0, AdjD 98.1).
South Florida ratings: KenPom No. 50 (AdjEM +15.77, AdjO 117.7, AdjD 101.9); Torvik No. 50 (AdjO 117.2, AdjD 102.3).
Recent reporting indicated Louisville starting point guard Mikel Brown Jr. had missed four games with a reaggravated back injury and his status was uncertain entering the week.
Published odds previews listed Louisville as about a 5.5-point favorite, supporting the Cardinals as the likely winner but not in a no-doubt matchup.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Mikel Brown Jr. injury update Louisville vs South Florida NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Schedule NFL News Draft Fantasy Odds Betting Newsletter USA TODAY Sports Will Mikel Brown Jr. play in Louisville vs USF NCAA Tournament game? Brooks Holton Louisville Courier Journal Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville player health is priority for Pat Kelsey ahead of March Madness Ahead of NCAA Tournament, Louisville coach Pat Kelsey says the number one priority is making sure players are healthy — including Mikel Brown Jr. Louisville basketball has been without starting point guard Mikel Brown Jr. for the past four games due to a reaggravated back injury. The freshman, who's projected to be an NBA lottery pick later this year, is the Louisville Cardinals' No. 2 scorer and top facilitator. Will Brown return for UofL's first-round NCAA Tournament game Thursday vs. South Florida? Here's the latest update. Will Louisville basketball 's starting point guard, Mikel Brown Jr. , return from a reaggravated back injury to play in its NCAA Tournament opener against South Florida ? Coach Pat Kelsey didn't know for certain when he met with reporters Monday morning, three days before the sixth-seeded Cardinals battle the No. 11 Bulls in a 1:30 p.m. tipoff Thursday at KeyBank Center in Buffalo, New York: "He made great progress last week," Kelsey said. "We'll see how the next couple of days of practice go." Brown, a freshman who's projected to be an NBA lottery pick later this year, has been sidelined for UofL's past four games — up to 12 total on the season. Kelsey said he reaggravated the back injury, which initially surfaced in mid-December, by taking some hard falls during a Feb. 23 loss at then-No. 19 North Carolina . Brown last played during a Feb. 28 loss at Clemson : five points on 2-for-10 shooting, four assists, two rebounds and two steals across 21 minutes off the bench Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today! New to March Madness in 2026: injury reports . Louisville must submit an initial report to the NCAA by 9 p.m. Wednesday and provide updates by 11:30 a.m. Thursday. Players will be designated as available (more than a 75% chance to play), questionable (up to a 75% chance to play) or out (will not play). The reports will be available on NCAA.com . Last Wednesday at the ACC Tournament , Brown told reporters he was "starting to get there. I'm slowly progressing; I'm slowly doing more day in and day out, just trying to get a feel for it. But, obviously, like coach said, the plan is: I'll come back when I'm 100%." When asked, "Are you 100% positive that you’ll be back for the NCAA Tournament?" He replied, "Right now, I’m just focused on my rehab more than anything. When the time feels right, I'm going to talk to the coaching staff; and we’re going to come to an agreement." Louisville basketball's Mikel Brown Jr. provides back injury update Mikel Brown Jr. breaks down the back injury keeping him out of the ACC Tournament

result.text: Louisville vs South Florida picks for March Madness Skip to main content Home Sports Cardinals & NFL Suns & NBA ASU Sun Devils UA Wildcats GCU Antelopes High Schools Sports On TV D-Backs & MLB Cactus League Louisville vs South Florida picks, predictions, odds for March Madness Jeremy Cluff Arizona Republic Show Caption Hide Caption March Madness Final Four predictions and national champion picks From chalk favorites to hot upsets, the panel shares Final Four picks and bold calls on who cuts down the nets. No. 6 seed Louisville and No. 11 seed South Florida play Thursday, March 19, in a March Madness NCAA Tournament first-round game . The East Region game is scheduled for 10:30 a.m. MST and can be seen on TNT ( stream with Sling TV ). Who will win the March Madness game and advance to the second round in the NCAA Tournament bracket ? Check out these March Madness picks and March Madness predictions for the men's college basketball NCAA Tournament matchup . Louisville is a 5.5-point favorite over South Florida in the game in March Madness odds provided by BetMGM Sportsbook . The Cardinals are -225 on the moneyline. The Bulls are +185. The over/under for the game is set at 165.5 points. Can the Bulls spring an upset as a 5.5-point underdog? Yes, they can." March Madness picks: Predictions for NCAA Tournament first-round games Dimers : Louisville 83, South Florida 78 It writes: "After extensive simulations, our model gives South Florida a win probability of 34%, while Louisville has a win probability of 66%." Join the madness: USA TODAY Bracket Challenge | Survivor Pool | Printable bracket ESPN : Louisville has an 81% chance to beat USF The site gives the Bulls a 19% shot at defeating the Cardinals in the NCAA Tournament March Madness first-round game. Bookies.com : Bet South Florida with the points vs Louisville Bill Speros writes: "Louisville enters this tournament ranked in the top 25 in adjusted offense and defense. But the Cardinals are banged up, and coach Pat Kelsey has yet to win an NCAA Tournament game. South Florida rolls into Buffalo on a 12-game winning streak. The Bulls are tanned, rested, and ready." modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
